text: How many browser tabs is too many to have open at once?

saboteurs.metric: Error pages currently 'Not Found'
Tab playing music you can't find
Gigabytes of RAM screaming for mercy
Remaining patience for the spinning wheel
Bookmarked articles I will never read

text: How many 'I'm nearly ready' lies is it okay to tell before leaving?

saboteurs.metric: Snoozed alarms in a row
Lies told while still in my pyjamas
Soul-crushing realisation I'm going to be late
Minutes of staring into the void
Empty promises of 'leaving now!'

text: How many days is acceptable to leave a text on 'read'?

saboteurs.metric: Days until it's officially too awkward to reply
Seconds of peace before the follow-up '?'
Drafted replies that were too much effort
Read receipt that caused an existential crisis
Business days (if you're a professional ghost)

text: How many slices of cake is a reasonable amount at a birthday party?

saboteurs.metric: Slices left after the 'Birthday Kid' takes theirs
Thin slivers that technically equal one slice
Hands required to carry the plate
Percent chance of a sugar crash at 4:00 PM
Giant slab that replaces a real meal

text: How many times can you replay a song before it's officially ruined?

saboteurs.metric: Problems, but the song isn't one
Time is all it takes if it's 'Baby Shark'
Repeats before the lyrics lose all meaning
Hours of a 'Lofi Beats' loop
Angry knocks on the wall from the neighbours

text: How many minutes late is still considered 'on time' for a party?

saboteurs.metric: Minutes of 'Fashionable' lateness
Chances I'm the first one there
Minutes spent circling for a park at Indooroopilly
Hours if you're the one bringing the ice
Minutes of 'I'm around the corner' lies

text: How many pillows on a bed is officially 'too many'?

saboteurs.metric: Pillow actually used for sleeping
Decorative shams that live on the floor
Percent of the mattress currently invisible
Minutes spent 'unmaking' the bed to find the sheets
Room left for an actual human

text: How many 'one last episodes' can you watch at 1:00 AM?

saboteurs.metric: Stages of grief when the sun starts coming up
Season finale that was definitely worth the fatigue
Hours of sleep I've just negotiated away
Self-control remaining in the Netflix UI
Episodes if the 'Skip Intro' button is working

text: How many spider sightings in a house before you move out?

saboteurs.metric: Huntsman the size of a dinner plate
Laps of the house I'm doing while screaming
Legs moving way too fast for comfort
Real estate agents called immediately
Spiders that have started paying rent

text: How many years must pass before movie spoilers are fair game?

saboteurs.metric: Years, and people still haven't seen 'The Sixth Sense'?
Days if you're a monster on Twitter
Week (The International Grace Period)
Years until even the spoiler doesn't matter
Hours after the midnight screening

text: How many times can you say 'What?' before you pretend to hear them?

saboteurs.metric: Strikes and you're out (just nod and smile)
Panic-induced 'Haha, yeah' to a question
Degrees of head-tilting to look focused
Idea of what is actually being discussed
Percent chance they asked 'How are you?'

text: How many chips can you steal from someone's plate before it's a crime?

saboteurs.metric: Single chip that represents the 'Friendship Tax'
Chips if they are the 'crunchy' ones at the bottom
Evidence left behind on the tablecloth
Months in 'Snack Jail' for your crimes
Percent of the box 'borrowed' during the drive home

text: How many alarms do you need to actually get out of bed?

saboteurs.metric: Alarm that is physically across the room
Snooze cycles that feel like 2 minutes each
Minutes of 'scrolling' before my eyes actually open
Willpower available before 8:00 AM
Different songs that I now hate

text: How many degrees of 'spicy' is the perfect level for a curry?

saboteurs.metric: On a scale of 1 to 10
Litres of milk required to survive
Tastebuds remaining after the third bite
Sweaty napkins used to mop my forehead
Humble 'Mild' that was actually a 'Volcanic'

text: How many standard Tim Tams can a human eat in one sitting?

saboteurs.metric: Which is exactly one full packet
Tim Tam used as a straw for a Slam
If you've had a really bad day
Crumbs left in the tray when the kids find it
Percent of my daily calories in 5 minutes

text: How many consecutive days can you wear the same jeans before washing them?

saboteurs.metric: Wears before they can stand up on their own
Days until someone gives you 'The Look'
Day after sitting on a train seat you immediately regret
Days per year — technically a capsule wardrobe
Rotations per year if you own exactly two pairs

text: How many minutes on hold is the limit before it's okay to hang up?

saboteurs.metric: 'Your call is important to us' before the lie sinks in
Minutes of the same 8-bar jazz loop burned into my soul
Times I've called Telstra voluntarily
Steps in the choreography I've invented while waiting
Seconds before the line drops anyway

text: How many items in an online shopping cart is too many to actually buy?

saboteurs.metric: Items that survive a 24-hour 'sleep on it' test
Tabs open comparing the exact same product
Moment of clarity that strikes right before checkout
Items decomposing in my 'Saved for Later' graveyard
Different sizes of the same jeans, just in case

text: How many exclamation marks in a single work email is too enthusiastic?

saboteurs.metric: That somehow reads as shouting with a smile
Needed to sound passive-aggressive in plain text
Before HR does a quiet 'wellness check'
In 'Thanks!!!' before someone screenshots it
Percent chance they can feel my anxiety through the screen

text: How many 'quick tasks' can pile up before they become an actual Project?

saboteurs.metric: 'Five-minute job' that took three weekends
Hardware store trips this task requires
YouTube tutorials watched before any work begins
Tools currently in the right place when I need them
Items on the list that spawned their own sub-lists

text: How many times can you check the fridge before admitting there's nothing to eat?

saboteurs.metric: Identical open-stare-close cycles in a row
Container of leftovers I've moved past six times
Things I actually want in there
Times I've rearranged the condiments hoping for inspiration
Percent chance I order UberEats in the end

text: How many items can you buy before a 'quick trip to the shops' needs a trolley?

saboteurs.metric: Items that turned into a full weekly shop
Bags I remembered to bring from the car
Items that don't fit in two arms but I'll try anyway
Basket handle cutting off circulation by checkout
Impulse buys that justify getting the trolley in retrospect

text: How many hours on a YouTube rabbit hole is acceptable on a weeknight?

saboteurs.metric: Video about 'How Traffic Jams Form' that started everything
AM before I realise I've been watching drone footage of Greenland
Videos watched that I actually searched for
'Recommended' videos I've somehow already seen
Documentaries about topics I will never use in real life

text: How many days can leftovers stay in the fridge before they become questionable?

saboteurs.metric: Days until you do the Brave Sniff Test
Chance you remember what it was by day five
Days if it's sealed in original Tupperware (honour system)
Containers lurking in the back corner right now
Optimistic 'it's definitely fine' that definitely wasn't

text: How many apology texts is the right amount after accidentally ignoring someone?

saboteurs.metric: Texts sent because I panicked and called instead
Drafts deleted because I over-explained
Gif that says 'I'm so sorry' sent instead of actual words
Percent chance they've already forgotten about it
Follow-up '???' messages that arrived before my reply

text: How many plants is officially too many for one apartment?

saboteurs.metric: That are currently thriving without human intervention
Leaves blocking the only natural light in the living room
Cactus per ex-partner (therapeutic)
Dead ones I've quietly replaced hoping nobody noticed
That I've run out of names for

text: How many times should you re-read a message before hitting send?

saboteurs.metric: Re-reads before I change it back to what it originally said
Second I notice the typo after hitting send
Times the recipient reads it as carefully as I wrote it
Times read aloud to check if it 'sounds passive-aggressive'
People sent it to for a second opinion

text: How many steps on your phone in a day counts as 'being active'?

saboteurs.metric: Steps — a number someone invented in 1960 to sell pedometers
Steps to the fridge and back, repeated with conviction
Walk from the car to the shops I definitely counted
Steps that justify the second helping
Steps recorded because I left my phone on the couch all day

text: How many times can you refresh your inbox before admitting they're not going to reply?

saboteurs.metric: Refreshes in the first hour alone
Notification that turned out to be a Coles catalogue
Replies ever received from 'Do Not Reply' addresses
Days before the follow-up 'Just checking in!' message
Percent chance they're composing a reply right now (they're not)

text: How many bags of snacks is it acceptable to bring to a two-hour movie?

saboteurs.metric: Bag that makes a noise during every quiet moment
Smuggled items that actually made it through bag check
Dollars saved by bringing my own Maltesers
Layers of snacks hidden under a jacket
Percent chance I run out before the trailers end

text: How many times can you cancel plans before you're officially unreliable?

saboteurs.metric: Cancellations before you get the 'Are you okay?' text
Cancel on the day of that nobody forgets for a year
Times I've cancelled and actually felt fine about it
Backup excuses stored for a genuine emergency
Percent chance I was never going in the first place

text: How many cups of coffee per day is officially too many?

saboteurs.metric: Cup that gives you the shakes
Cups before your resting heart rate is a public health concern
Days without it since sometime in 2019
'Just a quick one' promises broken before lunch
Hours of sleep I owe myself for every one I drink

text: How many 'I'll do it tomorrow' days before a task is officially abandoned?

saboteurs.metric: Tomorrows until it quietly becomes someone else's problem
Day it mysteriously migrates to next year's resolutions
Tasks on the list that actually got done on day one
Days of guilt before I rename it 'optional'
Business days before I delete it with quiet relief

text: How many 'We should catch up!' texts until it just means 'never'?

saboteurs.metric: Texts sent with zero dates ever proposed
Time we actually caught up — briefly, at a mutual friend's thing
Years since the first 'We should really do it this time!'
Concrete plans that survived past Thursday
Percent chance the next message is also 'We should catch up!'

text: How many hours sleep is the minimum before you can still claim you 'slept fine'?

saboteurs.metric: Hours, followed by approximately eight coffees
REM cycles achieved on Christmas Eve
Times I woke up and checked my phone 'just quickly'
Hours that feel like 2 after a 1 AM 'one last episode'
Hours I confidently promise myself every Sunday night

text: How many 'Remind Me Later' taps before you just do the software update?

saboteurs.metric: 'Later' buttons pressed across twelve months
Update that broke everything — hence the ongoing hesitation
Times I've updated at a genuinely convenient moment
Years of battery health sacrificed to avoiding iOS 17
Percent chance it's 'Bug fixes and performance improvements'

text: How many songs can you skip before Spotify decides you have commitment issues?

saboteurs.metric: Skips before the free tier cuts you off mid-chorus
Songs listened to all the way through this week
Songs skipped while 'looking for something to match my mood'
Song I skip every time but refuse to remove from the playlist
Minutes spent skipping instead of just picking something

text: How many minutes of mindless scrolling before it counts as wasting your evening?

saboteurs.metric: Minutes before I already know I should stop
Minutes — basically a movie, but with no plot
Minutes of content I will not remember by morning
Reel about productivity watched while being unproductive
Minutes lost inside an argument I wasn't even involved in

text: How many times is it acceptable to return something to the shops?

saboteurs.metric: Returns that didn't involve mild shame at the counter
Trips back before staff start recognising your face
Item returned 'not quite right' that was definitely already worn
Months the receipt technically covers you, apparently
Items currently sitting in the boot waiting to go back

text: How many people in a group chat is too many before it becomes unmanageable?

saboteurs.metric: Person who never mutes it and gets every notification
Unread messages waiting after a ten-minute shower
Side chats started specifically to manage the main chat

People typing at once, and somehow nothing useful is said

text: How many 'Final Sales' can a store hold before it stops counting as a sale?

saboteurs.metric: Consecutive 'Biggest Sale of the Year' events in twelve months
'Last chance!' email received today alone

Percent off that somehow always ends up on the ugly stock
Times this store has been 'closing down for good' since 2018

text: How many hours of binge-watching before you owe yourself an outdoor walk?

saboteurs.metric: Episode where I said 'just one more' and genuinely meant it
Hours until the streaming service sends a 'Still watching?' check
Steps taken since the couch became my permanent address
Seasons in and I can't remember the first character's name
Hours on a rainy Sunday — spiritually this is fine

text: How many grocery bags can you carry from the car in one trip?

saboteurs.metric: Trip I am physically and emotionally committed to making
Bags cutting off all circulation below the elbow
Times I've accepted help when offered
Bags I dropped on the driveway and refused to acknowledge
Percent chance I attempt it regardless of the answer

text: How many days without checking the news before it feels irresponsible?

saboteurs.metric: Days until a friend's 'Did you hear about...' sends me into a spiral
Headlines this week that improved my morning
Day off ruined by accidentally seeing a push notification
Days recommended by every therapist I've spoken to
News tabs opened, briefly glimpsed, and immediately closed

text: How many candles burning at once is the correct amount for a relaxing atmosphere?

saboteurs.metric: Lighters found when I actually want to light them
Candles that make the room a legitimate fire hazard
That I forgot about until the smoke alarm reminded me
Dollars spent on a candle that smells like 'rain on concrete'
Different scents competing in a 'smells like a headache' symphony

text: How many times can you google a symptom before you're convinced it's serious?

saboteurs.metric: Search before 'rare tropical disease' appears in the results
Results that concluded I was probably fine and should go to bed
AM emergency room visits that turned out to be dehydration
Distinct conditions I definitely have based on a mild headache
Browser tabs open, each with an increasingly grim prognosis

text: How many days can dishes sit 'soaking' before it stops being soaking?

saboteurs.metric: People in this house who share my definition of 'soon'
Days until the water develops its own functioning ecosystem
Bowl of cereal I will never publicly acknowledge
Passive-aggressive notes left on the kitchen bench before I snapped
